meta.title: How Much Does a Website Cost? A Honest Breakdown for Small Businesses
meta.description: What does a website actually cost in 2026? Alex, web developer in Rome, breaks down real pricing, hidden fees, and what ownership actually means.

title: How Much Does a Website Cost?
lead: A professionally built website for a small business typically costs between 500 and 5,000 euros. Most end up in the 1,000 to 2,500 range. That range is almost useless without context, though. This guide breaks down what you are actually paying for, what you are probably overpaying for, and what it really costs to get a site that works.

sections.heading: Why website prices vary so wildly
paras: The biggest driver is who builds it and how.

A 150 euro quote usually means a pre-built template with your logo dropped in, hosted on a platform you do not own. It looks like a website. It will not rank on Google. It will not load fast on mobile. And the moment you stop paying the monthly fee, it disappears.
An agency quote of 10,000 euros or more often includes project managers, account managers, weekly calls, and brand strategy workshops. You are paying for their overhead, not your results.
A freelance developer builds a custom site from scratch. No bloat, no recurring fees to them, no template. For most small businesses, that is exactly what they need.

sections.heading: What you are actually paying for
paras: Design: not just how it looks, but how it guides a visitor toward contacting you. Every colour, every button placement, every headline is a decision that affects whether someone calls you or leaves.
Code quality: a fast site (loading under 2 seconds) is not an accident. It is clean code, optimised images, caching, and a proper hosting setup. Google measures load speed. Slow sites rank lower.
SEO foundation: title tags, meta descriptions, structured data, sitemap, Google Search Console setup. These do not guarantee the top result on Google. Without them, you are essentially invisible.
Mobile: over 70% of visitors to a small business website arrive on a phone. A site that looks wrong on mobile loses those visitors in under 3 seconds.
Ownership: you own the result, or you do not. This is the part most people skip past and later regret.

sections.heading: The hidden costs nobody mentions upfront
paras: Domain name: 10 to 15 euros per year. You pay this regardless of who builds your site.
Hosting: 5 to 25 euros per month depending on provider. Some developers handle this for you and mark it up. Others build on platforms like Vercel where basic hosting is free.
CMS fees: if your site is built on Squarespace or Wix, you pay a monthly subscription (13 to 40 euros) forever. Stop paying, the site goes down.
Update charges: some agencies charge 50 to 150 euros to change a phone number or swap a photo. Ask directly before signing anything: who can make changes, and what does it cost?

Plugin renewals: WordPress sites often depend on paid plugins for SEO, bookings, and forms. Each renews annually. A site that costs 800 euros to build can accumulate 300 euros per year in plugin fees within two years.

sections.heading: What owning the code actually means
paras: When I build a website, you receive everything at launch: the source code, the domain, hosting access, and the design files. You are not renting anything from me.

You can move to a different host without rebuilding anything. You can hire any developer to make changes, not just me. There is no monthly fee to keep the site online. If I disappeared tomorrow, your website would still be running.
This is how it should work. It is not standard across the industry, so ask explicitly before you commit.

sections.heading: When does it cost more?
paras: Booking systems with payments: integrating a payment processor, calendar logic, and automated confirmations takes significantly more time than a static site.
Multiple languages: three language versions means three sets of every page, plus a language detection system. I build multilingual sites frequently.
Custom animations: time-intensive to do well.
E-commerce: a product catalogue, cart, and checkout is a completely different scale of work.
Tight deadlines: a five-day turnaround is possible but costs more than a two-week timeline.
If none of these apply to you, a professional five-page business website should not cost more than 2,000 euros.

costTable.heading: What does a website cost to run after launch?
costTable.intro: For a custom site on modern hosting infrastructure:
rows: Per hour, only when you ask
costTable.note: Most small business sites I build cost under 15 euros per year to keep running after launch.

items.q: Can I get a website for under 500 euros?
items.a: Yes, but manage expectations. At that price you are getting a template, minimal customisation, and usually a platform you will pay monthly for. It is fine as a starting point. It will not generate leads.

items.q: Do I need to pay monthly for a website?
items.a: Not always. Platform-based sites like Squarespace and Wix require a monthly subscription. A custom-built site on modern hosting infrastructure does not. Ask your developer upfront which approach they use.

items.q: How long does it take to build a website?
items.a: For a standard small business site: one to two weeks from content ready to launch. Delays almost always come from waiting on text, photos, and client feedback, not from the development itself.

items.q: Is a cheap website ever the right choice?
items.a: For a brand-new business that needs something live while you find your footing, yes. A 200 euro Squarespace site is better than nothing. Once you have clients and something to say, invest in a proper site.

items.q: What is the difference between a freelancer and an agency?
items.a: Scale and overhead. An agency employs project managers, designers, developers, and account teams. You pay for all of them. A freelancer is the person who actually builds the site. For a small business, a good freelancer typically delivers the same result at a fraction of the cost.

items.q: Will my website show up on Google?
items.a: A properly built site with an SEO foundation will be indexed by Google within days of launch. Ranking well for competitive terms takes months of real-world traffic data. Anyone who guarantees you the top position is misleading you.

items.q: Do I need a blog to rank on Google?
items.a: Not to rank for your business name or local searches. A blog helps if you publish content your potential clients are actively looking for. For most small businesses, three well-written guide articles outperform weekly posting for a year.

items.q: How do I know if the price is fair?
items.a: Ask for an itemised quote: design, development, SEO setup, content, and what is included after launch. A developer who works transparently will give you this without hesitation. Vague pricing is usually a sign of vague work.

authorBio: Written by Alex, web developer based in Rome, Italy. I build custom websites for small businesses across Italy and Europe.
authorCta: Get a free quote on WhatsApp
